title: Joke with delayed punchline
question: Display a joke, revealing the punchline only after the user presses Enter.
code:
import random
jokes = {
    "Why don't scientists trust atoms?": "Because they make up everything!",
    "Why did the programmer quit his job?": "Because he didn't get arrays!",
    "Why do Python devs prefer snakes?": "Because they don't need braces!",
}
setup, punch = random.choice(list(jokes.items()))
print(setup)
input("Press Enter for the punchline... ")
print(punch)
expected_output:
Why do Python devs prefer snakes?
Press Enter for the punchline... Because they don't need braces!
note: Joke is chosen at random, so the output changes every run.

title: Days left in current month
question: Read today's date (day part) and show how many days are left in the current month.
code:
import datetime, calendar
today = datetime.date.today()
day = int(input("Enter today's date (day): "))
last = calendar.monthrange(today.year, today.month)[1]
print(f"{last - day} days left in {today:%B}.")
expected_output: Enter today's date (day): 15 days left in June.
note: Auto-detects the month, so output depends on the current date.

title: Output 5, 10, 9
question: Start from 5, double it to 10, subtract 1 to get 9 — print each on its own line.

title: Output 5@10@9
question: Same values as before, but printed as 5@10@9.

title: First five multiples (max 3 lines)
question: Assign the first five multiples of a number to five variables and print them.
technique: generator expression unpacked into five names
code:
n = int(input("Enter a number: "))
a, b, c, d, e = (n*i for i in range(1, 6))
print(a, b, c, d, e)

title: Area of a circle
question: Accept the radius of a circle and print its area.
code:
import math
r = float(input("Enter radius: "))
print(f"Area = {math.pi * r**2:.2f}")

title: Average of five marks
question: Accept marks in five subjects and output the average.
code:
from statistics import mean
marks = [float(x) for x in input("Enter 5 marks (space-separated): ").split()]
print(f"Average = {mean(marks):.2f}")

title: Height: cm to feet & inches
question: Ask for height in centimetres and convert to feet and inches.
technique: divmod for quotient+remainder in one call
code:
cm = float(input("Enter height in cm: "))
feet, inches = divmod(cm / 2.54, 12)
print(f"{int(feet)} ft {inches:.1f} in")
expected_output: Enter height in cm: 5 ft 6.9 in

title: n squared, cubed, fourth
question: Read a number n and print n^2, n^3 and n^4.
technique: generator of f-strings, unpacked with sep
code:
n = int(input("Enter a number: "))
print(*(f"n^{p} = {n**p}" for p in (2, 3, 4)), sep="\n")
expected_output:
Enter a number: n^2 = 9
n^3 = 27
n^4 = 81

title: Area of a triangle (Heron's)
question: Find the area of a triangle.
technique: Heron's formula, math.sqrt, unpacking three sides
code:
import math
a, b, c = (float(x) for x in input("Enter three sides (space-separated): ").split())
s = (a + b + c) / 2
print(f"Area = {math.sqrt(s*(s-a)*(s-b)*(s-c)):.2f}")

title: Simple & compound interest
question: Compute simple interest and compound interest.
code:
p, r, t = (float(x) for x in input("Enter principal, rate, time (space-separated): ").split())
si = p * r * t / 100
ci = p * ((1 + r/100)**t - 1)
print(f"Simple Interest = {si:.2f}\nCompound Interest = {ci:.2f}")
expected_output:
Enter principal, rate, time (space-separated): Simple Interest = 100.00
Compound Interest = 102.50

title: First five multiples
question: Input a number and print its first five multiples.
code:
n = int(input("Enter a number: "))
print(*(n*i for i in range(1, 6)))

title: Student details, two ways
question: Read name, class, age; print them on one line, then on separate lines with two blank lines between.
technique: comprehension to gather inputs, unpacking + str.join
code:
fields = [input(f"Enter {k}: ") for k in ("name", "class", "age")]
print(*fields)
print("\n"*2 + "\n".join(fields))
expected_output:
Enter name: Enter class: Enter age: Atul 11 16


Atul
11
16

title: Replace first two with sums
question: Read three numbers; replace the first two with (first+second) and (second+third).
code:
a, b, c = (int(x) for x in input("Enter three numbers (space-separated): ").split())
print("Before:", a, b, c)
a, b = a + b, b + c
print("After:", a, b, c)

title: Profit earned
question: Input cost price and selling price, print the profit.
code:
cp, sp = (float(x) for x in input("Enter cost and selling price (space-separated): ").split())
print(f"Profit = {sp - cp:.2f}")
expected_output: Enter cost and selling price (space-separated): Profit = 50.00

title: Debt-to-Equity ratio
question: Input total liabilities and shareholders' equity, print the D/E ratio.
code:
liab, equity = (float(x) for x in input("Enter liabilities and shareholders' equity: ").split())
print(f"D/E Ratio = {liab / equity:.2f}")
expected_output: Enter liabilities and shareholders' equity: D/E Ratio = 2.00

title: Equity multiplier
question: Input total assets and total equity, print the equity multiplier.
code:
assets, equity = (float(x) for x in input("Enter total assets and total equity: ").split())
print(f"Equity Multiplier = {assets / equity:.2f}")
expected_output: Enter total assets and total equity: Equity Multiplier = 4.00

title: Gross / net profit & percentage
question: Accept COGS, revenue and operating costs; print gross profit, net profit and net profit %.
code:
cogs, rev, oc = (float(x) for x in input("Enter COGS, revenue, operating costs: ").split())
gross = rev - cogs
net = gross - oc
print(f"Gross Profit = {gross:.2f}\nNet Profit = {net:.2f}\nNet Profit % = {net/rev*100:.2f}%")
expected_output:
Enter COGS, revenue, operating costs: Gross Profit = 70000.00
Net Profit = 50000.00
Net Profit % = 41.67%
